gloss: to come near, approach, arrive at; to reach, attain, enter into any state or condition (e.g. bondage, fixation); to fall into, incur
four (often in compounds, e.g. four fingers broad, four palas, fourfold)
giving, yielding, bestowing, granting; causing, producing, effecting (mostly at the end of compounds)
an auspicious and inceptive particle: now, then, moreover, thereupon, afterwards; but, however, rather; often beginning a work or chapter (athātaḥ, now therefore)
to touch, feel with the hand, lay the hand on; to come into contact with; to reach, arrive at; to affect, concern; to touch (water) for purification
to read aloud, recite, repeat; to read, study; to mention, cite, teach, declare (in a text)
a town, city (also m.); nagara-vāsin, an inhabitant of a city
to smear, besmear, anoint, plaster, coat with; to stain, defile, pollute; (passive) to be attached to, cling to
to shine, look beautiful or handsome, be bright, splendid or radiant; to beautify, embellish, adorn
to leave, abandon, quit, forsake; to give up, renounce, resign, avoid, shun; to let go, dismiss, discharge
eight
to declare, announce, relate, say, speak; to cite, quote, illustrate; udāhṛta, called, named, designated, declared
to take hold of, take, seize, accept, receive (especially gifts); to welcome, receive kindly or hospitably; to take (a wife), marry
always, ever, at all times, continually, perpetually
to hurry, hasten, make haste, be quick; tvarita, swift, quick, expeditious; tvaramāṇa, hastening, hurrying
to call, call upon, summon, invoke, invite, challenge
to anoint, smear with, besmear (especially with ointment, ghee, blood etc.); to decorate, adorn; to honour, celebrate; akta, anointed, smeared, mixed with
to go, walk, proceed, travel, wander; to go to, approach; to pass into, attain, come to (a state or condition, e.g. equality, decay); to depart
the Soma plant and its pressed juice, the beverage of the gods offered in sacrifice; the moon or god of the moon; N. of various plants and medicinal herbs
an enclitic particle joined with a present tense to give it the sense of the past; indeed, certainly, verily, surely
to join or fasten together, unite, connect; to endow or furnish with; saṃyukta, joined, united, combined, connected with, furnished with, accompanied by
to go forwards, set out, enter, resort to, arrive at; to attain, obtain, reach (a state or condition); to take refuge with, throw oneself upon; to fly to for protection
to chew, bite, eat, devour, feed on, consume
a furrow, the track or line of the ploughshare; the furrow personified as a goddess; N. of the daughter of Janaka, king of Videha, and wife of Rāma
to throw or cast into, hurl upon; to put or place in, insert, deposit; to throw in, add, mix in
to remain standing, stand still; to stay, abide, remain, continue in a place or state; to settle; avasthita, standing, stationed, placed, remaining, steady, fixed
to look at, behold, see, regard, observe, view; to consider, examine, perceive, discern
to do honour to, treat well or with respect, honour, receive hospitably, entertain; to adorn; satkṛta, honoured, well-treated, hospitably received
to become clear or bright or placid; to become satisfied, pleased or tranquil; to be gracious or propitious, show favour; prasanna, clear, bright, pure, placid, gracious, well-disposed
to come, arrive, approach, come near or towards; to come back, return; to come to, reach, attain; āgata, come, arrived
always, constantly, regularly, ever, perpetually, daily
of two kinds, twofold, of two sorts, divided into two classes
to pervade, spread through, permeate, extend over, fill completely; to reach through, cover, occupy
to meet, come together, join, unite, combine; to mix, blend, amalgamate (also of metals and substances); to encounter, assemble
eating, feeding on, living on (at the end of compounds, e.g. eating fruits and roots, eating unwholesome food)

to turn out well, succeed, prosper, be accomplished or completed; to become, be turned into, fall to one's share; to arise, be produced; (as f. noun) success, prosperity, wealth, excellence, perfection, abundance
to rejoice, be glad, exult, be delighted or overjoyed; prahṛṣṭa, delighted, pleased, glad, thrilled with joy
to fly down, fall down, descend, alight; to fall upon, throw oneself down; to fall (in battle), drop down, collapse

to move, walk, go about, wander, roam; to graze; to behave, conduct oneself; to practise, perform, observe, undertake (a vow, penance, expiation); to be engaged in, carry on; (in alchemy) to be absorbed, penetrate
to go forth, set out, advance; to depart, die, decease; pretya, having died, after death, in the next world; preta, departed, dead, a deceased person, ghost
an eyebrow, the brow; bhrū-madhya, the space between the eyebrows
to send or pour forth, discharge, emit, shoot, cast, hurl; to let go, release, set free; to utter (sounds, a cry); to abandon, quit, leave, give up; to bestow, grant, dismiss
anger, rage, wrath, fury, passion
now, at present, at this time
to be born or produced, spring up, arise, come into being, be generated; to beget, generate, procreate, bring forth
to strike, beat, smite, hit; to kill, slay, destroy; to destroy, remove, counteract, cure (a disease, humour or poison)
resembling, like, having the appearance, colour or lustre of (mostly at the end of compounds, e.g. gold-coloured, fire-coloured); shining, splendid
to die, decease, perish; (in alchemy, of quicksilver and metals) to be killed, deadened or fixed; mṛta, dead, deceased; calcined (of metals)
to stretch out, extend, lengthen; to draw (a bow), stretch, strain; to restrain, hold in, stop; āyata, stretched out, extended, long, large
to practise, perform, do, act, undertake, carry out; to behave, conduct oneself towards; to observe, follow (a custom, usage or rule)
to receive, accept, take to oneself, appropriate; to take up, take along, bring with; upādāya, taking with, together with, including; beginning with, since
copper (especially in alchemy and medicine); a cord, string; a rule or manual of sacrificial measurement (śulba-sūtra)
to come to, go towards, approach, reach; to take place, occur, be suitable, fit, possible or right; upapanna, endowed with, furnished with, possessed of; suitable, fit, proper
to tremble, waver, be agitated, disturbed or distressed; to be pained, afflicted, grieved or frightened; vyathita, pained, distressed, troubled, alarmed
to ask, beg, solicit, entreat, implore, request (a person for a thing); to demand, require; to ask in marriage
to hold, bear, carry, support, sustain, maintain; to keep, retain, preserve; to wear; to hold up (the earth, a burden, an embryo); dhṛta, held, borne, carried, kept
to guard, watch, protect, defend, preserve, keep, save or deliver from; to take care of, tend
a community, nation, people (as opposed to the sovereign); a country, district, inhabited territory, realm; the inhabitants of a country
double, twofold, twice as much or as many, twice as large; multiplied by two
a Dānava, a class of demons descended from Danu, enemies of the gods; a demon, giant, Titan
a seer, inspired sage, singer of sacred hymns, holy poet; a saint, anchorite, ascetic; one of the seven great sages (maharṣi); the Ṛṣis as a distinct class of beings
to point out, indicate, show; to teach, instruct, advise; to declare, mention, prescribe, enjoin; upadiṣṭa, taught, prescribed, pointed out, indicated
royalty, kingship, sovereignty, kingly rule or government; a kingdom, realm, dominion, country
this, this here, referring to something near or present; this world, all this; (with particles) here, now, thus
to reach, attain, obtain, gain, acquire, get; to arrive at, meet with, overtake; to pervade, occupy; āpta, obtained, attained; trustworthy, fit
when, at what time, whenever; yadā ... tadā, when ... then, as soon as
firewood, fuel, kindling wood, a log or faggot for the sacred fire; samit-pāṇi, holding fuel in the hand
knowledge, science, learning, scholarship, lore; a spell, magical formula or incantation; the branches of knowledge (traditionally eighteen)
to overcome, overpower, conquer, surpass, subdue, predominate over; to attack, humiliate; abhibhūta, overcome, overpowered, subdued, afflicted by
to set out, depart, start, proceed, advance, go or march forth, set forward on a journey; prasthita, set out, departed, on the way to
phlegm, mucus, the phlegmatic humour (one of the three humours of the body in medicine)
long pepper, Piper longum (the plant and its fruit, much used in medicine)

to remember, recollect, bear in mind, call to mind, think of; to hand down or declare by tradition, teach; smṛta, remembered; recorded or prescribed in the traditional texts, declared, termed, called, regarded as
the thigh, shank
battle, war, fight, combat, conflict, contest
a season, period or division of the year (usually six); the fit, proper or appointed time; the menstrual period of women, the time favourable for conception (ṛtu-kāla)
in front, before, ahead, forward; in the presence of, before (with gen.); first, at the head, in the beginning
duly, properly, rightly, correctly, exactly, according to rule or precept; as it is, in due order, fitly
the stem of the first personal pronoun (I, me): mama, of me, mine; mayi, in me; mat-, in compounds, my, of me
to praise, laud, extol, eulogize, celebrate in song or hymns, glorify, worship with hymns
hunger
the privative or negative prefix (a- before consonants, an- before vowels), expressing negation, absence, contrariety: not, un-, without, -less
disagreeable, unpleasant, displeasing, disliked, unkind; m. an enemy, foe; n. anything unpleasant, an unkind word or deed
having a body, embodied, corporeal; m. a living being, creature, man; the embodied soul, the spirit enveloped in a body
covetousness, greed, cupidity, avarice; eager desire for, longing after; allurement, temptation
the Hotṛ priest who recites the invocations and hymns at the sacrifice (one of the four chief officiating priests); an offerer of oblations, sacrificer
an elephant (literally drinking twice, with trunk and mouth)
standing, staying, abiding, remaining; being situated in, existing or contained in, occupied with (at the end of compounds)
a star; a lunar mansion or asterism (one of the 27 or 28 divisions of the moon's path), constellation
the knee
cough, catarrh (a disease of the respiratory organs)
a Rākṣasa, demon, goblin, evil or malignant spirit, fiend (a class of beings who haunt the night, disturb sacrifices and devour men); relating to or fit for demons
a tree
dwelling, living, abiding, staying in, inhabiting (mostly at the end of compounds, e.g. forest-dwelling, mountain-dwelling)
a father; (dual) father and mother, parents; (plural) the fathers, forefathers, ancestors, the Manes or deified progenitors to whom oblations are offered
to fight, do battle, wage war, contend, combat, struggle with, oppose in battle
an oblation, burnt offering, anything offered in the sacrificial fire (as clarified butter, milk, soma, grain); clarified butter, ghee
to be satisfied, satiated, pleased or content; to satisfy oneself with, enjoy to the full; (causative) to satisfy, satiate, gratify, refresh (e.g. the Manes with offerings)
a merchant, trader, tradesman, dealer
to sit or lie down, settle down, take a seat, recline upon; to sink down, be dejected; niṣaṇṇa, seated, sitting, resting or leaning upon
highly fortunate, illustrious, eminent, distinguished, glorious, venerable, exalted (often in respectful address)
from behind, behind, in the rear, at the back (of); after, afterwards; westward
hatred, dislike, aversion, enmity, hostility, repugnance, antipathy
bringing, bringing about, producing, causing (at the end of compounds, e.g. bringing happiness, causing fear, bringing victory)
to fly or rush towards, come flying or darting on, fall upon, attack, assail; to approach suddenly, befall, happen; āpatat, rushing on, flying at
to point out, indicate, designate, specify; to declare, mention, prescribe, enjoin; to aim at, intend; uddiśya, aiming at, with reference to, towards, for the sake of
resembling, like, similar to, having the appearance of (at the end of compounds, e.g. fire-like, parrot-beak-coloured)
both (used in the dual, ubhau, ubhe)
to lick, lick up, lap, taste; to take as a linctus or electuary (a medicinal paste licked up with honey or ghee)
a forest, grove, wood
equal to, like, similar, comparable, of the same kind, rank or value as (with instrumental or in compounds)
within, inside, in the interior or middle of; between, amongst; antar-jala, being within the water
a man, human being; (plural) men, mankind
a mountain, hill, rock, elevation
a Brāhman, a man of the first or priestly caste; n. the Brāhmaṇa portion of the Veda (ritual and explanatory texts)
a deity, divinity, god, godhead; the image of a deity; divine rank or dignity
first, primary, original, being at the beginning, principal; (at the end of compounds) beginning with, and the rest, and so on, et cetera
belonging, relating or consecrated to fire or the god Agni, fiery; the fiery weapon (āgneya astra); the south-east quarter (presided over by Agni)
gravel, grit, pebbles, small stones; candied or granulated sugar; (in medicine) gravel or stone in the bladder (a disease)
a watch, a period of three hours, the eighth part of a day (especially of the night); restraint, cessation; N. of a class of deities
deep (of water, sound, voice etc.); deep-sounding, deep-toned; profound, grave, serious, dignified; unfathomable, impenetrable, hard to overcome
the great god, N. of Śiva (also applied to Rudra and other supreme deities)
command, order, edict, decree, direction; instruction, teaching, discipline; government, dominion, rule, sway; a royal grant or charter
a Brāhman, priest, sage; an inspired or wise man; vipra-indra, chief or best of Brāhmans
kind, sort, class, species; manner, mode, way, fashion; nānā-prakāra, of various kinds, manifold
a garden, park, pleasure-grove, royal garden (attached to a palace or city)
mixed, mingled, blended, combined, united with, accompanied by (with instrumental or in compounds); miscellaneous, diverse

nature, natural form, original or normal condition or state; (in Sāṃkhya philosophy) the primordial matter, the primary material cause of the world (reckoned as eightfold); the normal state or constitution (of the body); (plural) the subjects of a king, the citizens; the ministers
a horse, steed, stallion
semen, seed, sperm, seminal fluid; a flow or stream (of anything)
a solitary, lonely or retired place, seclusion; ekānte, apart, aside, privately, in a lonely spot; exclusive devotion to a single object; ekānta-, absolute, exclusive, invariable, devoted to one aim
purifying, cleansing, purification, refining (of the body, of metals, of quicksilver); a purgative or evacuant remedy; correcting, removal (of faults or impurities)
filled with, full of, crowded with (in compounds); confounded, agitated, distressed, perplexed, flurried, disordered
a particular weight of gold or silver (equal to sixteen māṣas, about 176 grains troy), much used in medicine and alchemy; drawing, dragging, pulling; ploughing
a chariot, car, especially a two-wheeled war-chariot; ratha-aṅga, a chariot wheel
an army, host, troop, forces, array of troops; front, face, appearance; edge, point, splendour
pain, sorrow, suffering, grief, distress, uneasiness, trouble, difficulty
possessing or riding in a chariot; m. a chariot-warrior, one who fights from a chariot, charioteer
a thing, object, article, substance; matter, subject-matter, contents (of a work); the real thing, reality; goods, property

being within range or reach of, accessible or perceptible to; frequenting, dwelling or living in (at the end of compounds, e.g. living in the forest); m. pasture ground, range, field of action, sphere, province, object of perception
wandering or roaming about; giddiness, dizziness, vertigo (a disease); confusion, perplexity, error, delusion, mistake; a whirling flame; a whirlpool
made of, consisting or composed of, formed of, full of (a suffix forming adjectives at the end of compounds, e.g. made of clay, made of gold)
effort, exertion, endeavour, pains, care, zeal, activity; prayatnena, prayatnataḥ, with effort, carefully, diligently, zealously
with one's own eyes, before one's eyes, visibly, evidently, openly; in person, in bodily form, in very deed, actually, directly, immediately; the very, veritable
enmity, hostility, animosity, hatred, quarrel, feud, grudge, revenge
a descendant of Bhṛgu (especially Paraśurāma, also Śukra, Cyavana and others); relating to or derived from Bhṛgu

a net (for catching fish, birds, deer etc.), snare; a lattice, grating, window; a web, mesh, netting, network (of gold etc.); a multitude, collection, mass (of waves, rays, veins etc.); illusion, deception
behind, in the rear, from behind; westward, from the west; afterwards, later, thereupon, then, at last
the earth, soil, ground, land (also as a goddess); a place, spot, site, situation; the floor of a house, storey; territory, country
a mountain, mountain-range, hill, height, rock; N. of a Ṛṣi (companion of Nārada)
an enclitic particle emphasizing or generalizing the preceding word: even, also, just, indeed, at least; (in the Veda, after interrogatives) -ever, -soever, any
suddenly, in a moment, at once; repeatedly, again and again, frequently, constantly (especially muhur muhuḥ)
bow, obeisance, reverential salutation, adoration, homage (with dative: salutation or hail to, often in worship formulas)
a wooden vessel, bucket, tub, trough; a measure of capacity (four āḍhakas); N. of the military preceptor of the Kurus and Pāṇḍavas
better, superior, preferable, more excellent, more auspicious (comparative); n. the better lot, welfare, good fortune, bliss, the highest good, final beatitude
arising, springing or produced from, originating in (at the end of compounds); m. coming into existence, birth, production, origin, source
a creature, living being; a man, person, individual; (collectively or in plural) people, folk, race, tribe, the people, subjects; (at the end of compounds) the class or body of persons
remainder, rest, residue, what is left over (of food, oblations, an amount); end, conclusion, issue; N. of the thousand-headed serpent supporting the earth
grown up, full-grown, aged, old, advanced in years; senior, venerable, experienced, wise; m. an old man, an elder
former, prior, preceding, previous, earlier, first, ancient; eastern, to the east; (at the end of compounds) preceded or accompanied by; the aforesaid, before-mentioned
the hand; a ray of light (of the sun or moon); the trunk of an elephant; tribute, tax, toll, duty; (as adjective at the end of compounds) doing, making, causing, producing
a bow (the weapon of an archer)
a share, portion, part, division; a fractional part (e.g. a fourth, an eighth, as in medicinal and alchemical proportions); a degree (in astronomy); N. of an Āditya
water
fruit (of a tree or plant); result, consequence, effect, issue; reward, recompense, requital (of works); gain, profit, advantage; the blade or point (of a weapon)
light, not heavy; light in the stomach, easily digested; quick, swift, nimble, active; small, slight, minute, insignificant, trifling; low, mean, of little worth
to shine, gleam, glow, burn; to suffer violent heat or pain, grieve, mourn, be afflicted; (as feminine noun śuc) grief, sorrow, pain, affliction
to go, proceed, move, walk, travel, march; to go to, reach, arrive at; to pass into, attain, come to (a state or condition, e.g. become liquid, come to destruction); to pass away, vanish, depart
N. of a river rising in the Vindhya mountains (mentioned among the rivers of the Deccan)
a virtuous or respectable woman of good family, a chaste high-born wife or matron
sandal-wood, the white or Malaya sandal (candana)
to shine over or beyond, outshine, surpass in brightness or splendour, be exceedingly brilliant
an ear-ornament, a large ring or disc worn in the lobe of the ear (chiefly by women)
N. of the wife of Śiva (Mṛḍa), Pārvatī, Durgā
cloudless, free from clouds, unclouded (of the sky)
very distant, very far off, remote; sudūram, very far, a great distance; sudūrāt, from afar
an actress, a dancing-girl or female performer belonging to a (royal or private) dramatic troupe
unfinished, not ended, not concluded (of speech or an affair); unsettled, undetermined; anavasitārtha, of unsettled or indeterminate meaning
to prove fruitless or vain, fail, be frustrated or foiled; to burst asunder, split open
relating to or derived from the sruva (sacrificial ladle); connected through sacrificial ties (of alliances, relationships or troops)
small cardamoms, the cardamom plant (elā); a very minute measure of quantity or time, an atom, an instant
a woman with gracefully bent or stooping limbs; N. of a medicinal plant, karkaṭaśṛṅgī (in lexicons)
active, industrious, energetic, persevering, laborious, exerting oneself, full of effort
wandering about as a religious mendicant, the life or order of a religious wanderer, renunciation, asceticism
most pleasant, most agreeable, most comfortable; running most easily (as a chariot)
N. of a medicinal plant, a species of gourd or Luffa (identified with the rāja-kośātakī or dhāmārgava)
south-eastern, lying between the east and the south
N. of Pārvatī or Durgā, the consort of Tryambaka (Śiva); also a kind of small insect (in ritual texts)
according to ability or power, to the best of one's capacity, as far as one is able
not to be taken or received, unacceptable; not to be admitted (as a plea or lawsuit), inadmissible; not to be captured or seized
fatigue, weariness, lassitude, exhaustion, languor
denial, disavowal, concealment (of what one knows), evasion; (in anatomy) N. of a vital spot (marman) on the side below the shoulder
night
the passing away of the heat, the end of the hot season, the beginning of the rains
a forest, wilderness, wild region; forest-life (of an ascetic or exile); relating to the forest
given to excessive sleep, oversleeping; atinidratā, excessive sleepiness, somnolence (as a morbid symptom)
to become much, many or abundant, increase, multiply; to spread, become widely diffused or public (as a report)
the southern direction, the south
to break down, break off, tear down, shatter, uproot (trees etc.); avarugṇa, broken down, snapped off, crushed
the ocean, the sea (receptacle of the waters)
a proud or offended woman, a heroine sulking from wounded pride or jealousy (in erotic poetry); a high-spirited woman
a rice-cook, one who prepares or sells boiled rice, a professional cook of rice dishes
not easy to obtain, hard to get, difficult of attainment, rare
what is proper or appropriate to be done; yathākāryam, according to what has to be done, as the business or occasion requires
the passing away of the clouds, the end of the rainy season, the autumn
the ten-necked one, N. of Rāvaṇa
rising and going forth to meet or welcome (a guest or superior), respectful reception
given, bestowed, presented, granted, distributed
meeting, union, coming together, contact, encounter; joining, combining, blending (also of substances in alchemy)
nearer, closer at hand, more imminent
having a daughter, possessed of daughters (especially of a father who receives the bride-price)
the enemy or slayer of the demon Mura, N. of Kṛṣṇa (Viṣṇu)
made into a circle or ring, rounded, bent round (as a bow drawn into a circle), coiled, curled
a lion-seat, lion-throne, royal throne (literally the seat of the king of beasts)
an underground passage, tunnel, mine, subterranean gallery (dug beneath walls or houses, e.g. by thieves or besiegers)
to pour upon or after, sprinkle over, besprinkle (with ghee, water etc., especially in ritual)
to carry, bear along, convey, bring (to a place); to carry down or home; to bear, support, sustain (a burden)
N. of an attendant of Śiva, the teacher of dancing from whom the tāṇḍava dance takes its name
of excessive size or measure, immense, huge, enormous, inordinately large
to go apart or asunder, diverge, separate; to pass through or between, traverse, pervade; viyāta, departed, gone away (as grief)
a female servant, maid-servant, slave-girl
the record-office, court of accounts and records, registry or archive of a king (where documents and account-books are kept)
a creeper, creeping or climbing plant, vine, spreading tendril
a fair-faced, lovely or beautiful woman; N. of various goddesses, women and plants; a favourite lady of the king (in lexicons)
a bad minister or counsellor, an evil adviser; having bad ministers, ill-advised
forming a pair, paired, coupled (as deities receiving a joint offering); one of a contending pair, an adversary or party in a (feigned) dispute or duel
a shampooer, one who kneads and rubs the limbs, a masseur; a carrier, bearer, conveyer
dwelling in heaven; m. a god, deity, celestial being
a mind-born daughter (created by thought); mental, spiritual (feminine of mānasa); N. of a Vidyādevī and other goddesses
crookedness, curvature, curliness; (of speech or glances) obliqueness, charming indirection, wit, ambiguity
claiming or arrogating to oneself, the conceiver of 'I' (the ego-principle, ahaṃkāra, as self-arrogating agent); one who covets or lays claim to; an injurer, insulter
the dead of night, midnight, the two middle watches of the night
one who administers poison, a secret poisoner (reckoned among treacherous attendants and spies); giving juice or flavour
trustworthy, reliable, confidential, enjoying confidence; (in law) a surety, one who stands bail or gives security
of great renown, whose fame is mighty (especially as an epithet of Indra)
putting or lulling to sleep, causing sleep, soporific; the magical sleep-weapon (astra) that lulls enemies to sleep; a sleep-charm or spell
having no other resort, refuge or recourse, entirely dependent on one person or thing
drawn away or downwards, dragged down; degraded, low, inferior, vile, base (of birth, conduct, mind); removed, taken away
free from anger or resentment, whose wrath has departed, appeased
to turn away or aside, turn back, withdraw, retire from; apāvṛtta, turned away, withdrawn, averted, removed (as a danger)
hearing, the act of hearing or listening (śrāvaṃ śrāvam, hearing again and again; amantra-śrāvam, without audible recitation of mantras)
undivided, unseparated, not different from, identical with, one and the same; unbroken
most bountiful, most gracious, most liberal (superlative of mīḍhvas; especially as an epithet of Śiva or Rudra)
contempt, humiliation, disgrace, insult, defeat; (in the Veda) superior or encompassing power, superiority
fanning, the act of fanning (with a palm-leaf or other fan); a fan
fearlessness, absence of fear (as the abhaya gesture of a deity's hand); absence of sunshine, shade (in lexicons); attack, onset (in the Veda)
one whose predictions come true, a prophetic astrologer, soothsayer, fortune-teller; the prediction of such a seer
the stage-manager or director of a drama (who holds the strings of the performance and speaks the prologue); a carpenter, architect, master-builder (holder of the measuring-line)
N. of a terrific goddess (in whose cremation-ground rites are performed); N. of a procuress; N. of various plants (in medical lexicons)
a lady's maid, female attendant who dresses and adorns her mistress, tire-woman; N. of a plant, cumin (in lexicons)
possessing a treasury, rich in treasure, wealthy (of a king); provided with a sheath, capsule or sac (of a tumour, in medicine)
a long line or bank of clouds, a mass or garland of clouds
according to space or room, wherever there is room, into the proper place; as occasion or opportunity offers
adorned with golden bracelets; N. of a king celebrated for his observance of the ekādaśī vow; N. of a warrior
pleasing, flattering or coaxing words, sweet talk, flattery, compliments (= cāṭu)
change, transformation, alteration (especially for the worse), the changefulness or decay inherent in all things; (in grammar) modification of gender or number in construing a word
N. of Brahmā; also an epithet of Indra ('having a hundred sacrifices or supports')
the month Pauṣa (December–January, when the full moon stands in the asterism Tiṣya or Puṣya)

expressed by the body or limbs, gesticulatory (one of the four modes of dramatic representation, alongside the vocal, the costume-based and the temperamental); bodily, corporeal; relating to members or subdivisions (as in the eightfold path)
an underground room or chamber, cellar, subterranean apartment (used for coolness or concealment)
a fire of straw or dry mats, a slow straw-fire (prescribed for the execution of certain criminals)
praise, eulogy, laudation, hymn of praise; worship, reverence
an assemblage or body of royal personages or kṣatriyas, the warrior nobility collectively
N. of the wife of Yama, the god of death
the forehead (= lalāṭa)
a pond, pool, lake, tank
born of or produced from the body: a son; a disease arising in the body; N. of Kāma, the god of love
the asterism Śatabhiṣaj ('requiring a hundred physicians'), the 24th lunar mansion, presided over by Varuṇa
the bodiless one, N. of Kāma, the god of love (whose body was consumed by Śiva); (adjective) very thin, slender, delicate
sick, ill, unwell, diseased (akalya-paricaraṇa, attendance on the sick); (in Śaiva philosophy, of time) indivisible, not made up of parts
sudden onset or approach, unexpected attack, swooping down upon; a sudden occurrence, accident, calamity that befalls
belonging to or derived from an elephant, elephantine (as an elephant's member, hide or measure)
the flowering time, the season of flowers, spring; the time of menstruation, the period favourable for conception
the sweet-voiced one: the Indian cuckoo (kokila); also a goose or pigeon (in lexicons); having a melodious throat or voice
produced or sprung from that, born of that, arising immediately therefrom; produced in that very place, native, indigenous (of gems etc.)
desire to know, wish to learn, curiosity, thirst for knowledge (especially tattva-bubhutsā, the desire to know the truth)
the treasurer-general, superintendent of the treasury and storehouses (who receives and deposits the collected revenue, in the Arthaśāstra); a receiver or harbourer (of stolen goods)
great heat, heating, glow (e.g. of milk over fire); excitement, agitation, impatience, ardour, distress
essentiality, substantiality, real worth or value, excellence, strength; the being the essence or core of anything
a young child, infant, boy; the young of an animal; a simpleton, fool (in lexicons); N. of an Asura
chief or lord of the munis, a great sage; especially N. of the Buddha (Śākyamuni); also of a Jina
a great calamity, misfortune, mishap, terrible event; excessive danger or fear; a daring, over-hazardous deed or venture
heat, glow, sunshine, the hot season; a ray of light, sunbeam
the lustre or splendour of jewels; N. of various women (a Vidyādharī heroine of the Kathāsaritsāgara, a merchant's wife etc.); N. of the first hell (in Jaina and Buddhist cosmology)
the front part, forepart, place or position in front, foreground; forwardness, obtrusiveness
the streak of gold made on the touchstone in assaying, the test-streak; the colour-grade or standard of fineness of gold (whose loss in alchemical operations is called bhañjanī)
lovely, beautiful, charming, pleasing, agreeable; sweet, melodious (of sounds, voices)
a hut, cottage, small house, hovel
the relation between co-wives, co-wifehood, rivalry of a fellow-wife; jealousy, rivalry, enmity (in general); belonging to or derived from a co-wife or rival
a row or multitude of stars; N. of various women (a Yakṣa maiden in the Daśakumāracarita, a Vidyādharī in the Kathāsaritsāgara)
a kind of partridge or francolin (a bird, = krakara); N. of a prince (a son of Nimi's line or a Vṛṣṇi)
wearing matted or braided locks (jaṭā); m. an ascetic with matted hair; N. of Śiva; N. of a lexicographer
placing or imposing upon; attributing, ascribing, superimposition (especially wrongly, as imputing faults to virtues); (in rhetoric and logic) metaphorical attribution, assumption
veiling, a veil, the covering of the face (worn by women); a covering, wrapper, shroud; hiding, concealment
spreading forth, diffusion, expansion; a mass, quantity, multitude, flood (of rays, lustre, nectar etc.)
blame, censure, reproach, disparaging or scandalous talk (of the people, jana-nirvāda); general condemnation
the spear-bearer, N. of Skanda (Kārttikeya), the god of war; bearing a spear or lance
wholly auspicious; N. of a Buddha or celebrated Bodhisattva; an epithet of the Buddha; N. of plants (in lexicons)
rich in honey, sweet, abounding in sweetness; N. of a river; N. of a city; N. of various plants (in lexicons); a stage of yogic attainment
licked, licked over, lapped, touched; n. the archer's lunging posture (right knee advanced, left leg drawn back), one of the standard shooting attitudes
N. of the mountain on which the river Yamunā rises (whence the Yamunā is called Kalinda's daughter); N. of a people; the Vibhītaka tree (in lexicons)
roaring, rumbling, thundering (especially of clouds), thunder; any inarticulate cry or sound
contradictory speech, altercation, wrangling, dispute; idle, incoherent or deceitful talk, prattle; lamentation, wailing
a noble, beautiful or high-spirited woman (often as a respectful form of address); about to be, future (feminine of bhāvin); N. of a goddess or Mātṛ
according to the sense or meaning, in fact, really, virtually, in reality; for the sake of wealth; as regards property or advantage
having a hundred joints or knots: an epithet of Indra's thunderbolt (vajra); (feminine) the bamboo, a knotted reed or grass, N. of plants (in lexicons)
the month Kārttika (October–November); manifold, various, abundant
relating to an object of sense, sensual, belonging to the sphere of the senses (e.g. flavour as the object proper to the tongue); worldly, material; relating to a particular sphere or province
a commander, ruler, orderer, chastiser (so in the Veda); command, order; a slaughtering-knife (in later texts)
want of regard or confidence, indifference, unconcern, disregard (for a thing); absence of attachment or interest
curved, bent, curled (especially of eyelashes and locks of hair); crooked
spreading out, stretching forth, extension, expansion, diffusion (of limbs, light, an army's line, commodities etc.)
a knife, dagger, small razor-like blade
N. of various princes and warriors (a son of Vasudeva, a Magadha king killed by his brother, and others)
a large bird of prey, = suparṇa; the mythical bird Garuḍa; (plural) a class of celestial bird-beings; N. of a tree (in lexicons)
N. of one of the attendants who stand at the side of the Sun; N. of a sage (author of a Sāṃkhya commentary); N. of a forest
the medicinal costus root, kuṣṭha (Saussurea lappa; so equated in the lexicons); m. the fever of an elephant
having an adamantine beak: an epithet of Garuḍa; a mosquito or stinging fly, also the milk-hedge plant sehuṇḍa (in lexicons)
exquisite beauty, perfect splendour, supreme loveliness, brilliance; (with the Jainas) N. of certain ages or spokes of the wheel of time
introduction, preface, exordium, opening of a topic; an apposite illustration, analogous instance, example brought forward
raising, lifting up; the ladling out or drawing up (of a libation into its vessel, in ritual); leading upwards; inference, conjecture, reasoning (in lexicons)
casting out, expulsion, removal, ejection; rejection, refutation; spitting or vomiting out; destruction, warding off (of afflictions)
kissing; touching closely, grazing, reaching to (at the end of compounds, e.g. touching the sunset mountain, brushing the ear)
the illuminator: the sun (in lexicons); N. of a king of Avanti (= Pradyota)
shining forth, diffusing light, brilliant; m. the Sun, the sun-god (father of Yama, Manu Vaivasvata and the Aśvins)
long-lived, destined to long life (used as an affectionate address to a child or dear person); the moon (in lexicons)
trembling, throbbing, quivering, twitching; flashing, sparkling, glittering; a gleam, flash, sudden outburst or manifestation
sand, a grain of sand (as in the sands of the Ganges); a young girl, servant-maid
old, aged, worn out, decayed; hard, harsh, fully ripe or matured (of plants grown past tenderness); grown intense, fierce (of sunlight or heat)
to be counted or reckoned, countable (gaṇya-paṇya, goods sold by count or number); to be reckoned or esteemed among, counted first (pūrva-gaṇya, foremost)
the mouth or opening of a cave, a cavern-mouth; N. of a monkey-chief (in the Rāmāyaṇa)
great bliss, supreme joy or rapture (in Tantric usage one of the ascending stages of mystic beatitude); N. of a sacred site and liṅga near Śrīśaila; N. of an attendant of Śiva
to fix firmly, prop, support, sustain; to stop, stiffen, paralyse, make rigid or immovable; stabdha, fixed, stiff, rigid, motionless, benumbed; obstinate, arrogant
to be agitated, startled or alarmed; āvigna, agitated, alarmed, distressed, perturbed, flurried
to desire, wish for, long for, strive after; abhīṣṭa, wished for, desired, agreeable, dear; a desired object, wish, aim
subject to another's will, dependent on another, in another's power; overpowered or dominated by, wholly under the influence of (in compounds, e.g. overcome by sleep, anger or fever)
forty-eight
a begetter, progenitor, father (feminine janayitrī, a mother)
to tremble with fear, be frightened, terrified or scared, flee in terror; vitrasta, frightened, terrified, alarmed
a sapphire (the dark-blue gem of Indra)
the hut or hamlet of Caṇḍālas or Śabaras, an outcaste or savage settlement
addressing, speaking to, accosting (priya-abhibhāṣin, speaking kindly; pūrva-abhibhāṣin, speaking first as a mark of courtesy)
Indra's bow, the rainbow
to become thoroughly tired, be fatigued or wearied, exert oneself greatly; pariśrānta, thoroughly tired, exhausted, worn out
to stretch or spread over, cover, overspread, extend along; to string together, connect, carry on, continue; saṃtata, overspread, covered with; continuous, uninterrupted
two or three, a few (used in the plural)
to sing together or in chorus, sing to (a person), celebrate in song; saṃgīta, sung in chorus; concerted song and music
a eunuch, an attendant or chamberlain of the women's apartments, harem-guard
to press hard, oppress, harass, afflict, torment, vex; to repel, drive back, ward off
(passive) to be visible, be seen, appear, become manifest; (active) to see, behold, perceive
to live upon, subsist by, depend upon for a living; to derive one's livelihood or profit from, make use of; upajīvya, a source of subsistence, one on whom others depend
a pregnant woman, one who has conceived (literally one to whom a living being has come)
searching after, seeking for, looking or inquiring for (at the end of compounds)
like you, of your kind, such as you, resembling your honour
welfare, well-being, comfort, happiness, health, ease, enjoyment, felicity
a worthless or trifling thing, an unworthy object; non-entity, unreality, that which has no real existence (in philosophy)
to scratch, scrape, tear up, furrow, graze; to write down, inscribe, delineate, draw, paint
N. of a medicinal cucurbitaceous creeper, a species of Luffa (equated with dhāmārgava and kośaphala in the lexicons)
excessive, exorbitant, beyond the proper measure; atyartham, excessively, exceedingly, very much, beyond measure
a guardian of the frontier, border-warden, keeper of the marches (a frontier commander or official)
to speak to, address, converse with, talk to; to utter, say
to rise up, come forth, go up, appear, spring or shoot up; udgata, risen, come forth, sprung up, towering, lofty (as a tall tree)
to attain fully, complete; paryāpta, complete, finished; sufficient, adequate, enough; equal to, a match for (in strength); abundant, ample, full
to throw up, cast up, raise, lift up, elevate; to throw out, cast away, expel, reject; udasta, thrown up, raised, lifted
to lean against or upon, rest on; to resort to, take refuge in, depend upon (niśritya, having resorted to); niśrita, leaning or resting on, dwelling in, dependent on
to sit round, surround; to attend or wait upon respectfully, serve, worship, honour, revere
sandal-wood, the sandal tree (especially the white sandal), much used as a fragrant paste and in medicine
despising, contemning; m. a despiser, contemner, scorner (of gods, gurus, Brāhmans etc.)
to go after, follow, accompany, attend; to conform to, obey, comply with (an order); (in grammar) to be construed or connected with, follow in sense
a jackal
according to the state, condition or circumstances (of the case, the patient or the disease)
an object of sense (sound, touch, form, taste, smell); the aim or gratification of the senses, sensual object or pleasure
to drive away, ward off, keep off, scare away, repel (evils, demons, enemies)
brass, a kind of brass or bell-metal alloy
fortunate, lucky, prosperous, happy, favoured by fortune
to pour upon or into, sprinkle over, infuse; to quench or temper (heated metal) by plunging into a liquid; niṣikta, poured on, sprinkled, quenched
to bind or fasten on, put on, gird on; pinaddha, bound on, fastened, tied on; covered with, wrapped or enveloped in, wearing
a kind of half-refined sugar, inspissated and granulated sugar-cane juice, coarse sugar candy (a stage in sugar refining)
a prefix (nis-) meaning out, forth, away; expressing privation, absence, freedom from or completeness: without, deprived of, -less, utterly
declaring, announcing, foretelling, indicating, boding (at the end of compounds, e.g. boding victory, boding evil); praising, reciting
the chiretta plant, Gentiana or Swertia chirata (a famous bitter medicinal herb, = bhūnimba, the 'bitter herb of the Kirātas')
a cucurbitaceous medicinal plant, the bristly luffa (= devatāḍa or devadālī), used as an emetic
instruction, direction, teaching, precept, advice; command, order, governance; a body of rules or doctrine (e.g. the teaching of the Veda or of yoga)
to run towards, run up to, rush at; to resort or betake oneself to (a deity or protector) for help, have recourse to
a female attendant, waiting-maid, servant-girl
to take hold of, seize, grasp; to take to wife, marry (upayeme, he married); to hold up, support
such, of such a kind or nature, such-like, of this sort
seeking, striving after, desiring, wishing for (at the end of compounds, e.g. well-wishing, desiring victory)
association, connection, combination, being together (with); literary or rhetorical composition, literature, belles-lettres, the science of poetics
a boy, lad, stripling; a young Brāhman student or religious novice (often used slightingly)
the pomegranate tree (feminine dāḍimī); n. the pomegranate fruit (sweet, acid and astringent, much used in medicine)
king of kings, supreme sovereign, emperor; N. of Kubera, the lord of wealth
sleepless, awake, wakeful, roused from sleep; (of flowers) opened, full-blown, expanded; (of hair) bristling, erect
daybreak, dawn, break of day, morning; (as adjective) begun to grow light, dawned
born of the same womb, uterine, of whole blood; m. a full or uterine brother, own brother
hungry, wishing to eat; desirous of worldly enjoyment (as opposed to the mumukṣu, the seeker of liberation)
wiping away, rubbing off, cleansing (e.g. the wiping away of tears); an instrument or appliance for wiping and cleaning (in surgery)
the collar-bone, clavicle; N. of a climbing plant or tree (in lexicons)
sound, noise, din, roar (of drums, clouds etc.); humming, murmuring
a shrub, bush, clump or thicket; the abdominal tumour-disease gulma (chronic enlargement of the spleen or other viscera); a squadron or military post; N. of a man
an earth-supporter: a mountain; a king, prince, sovereign
last, final, ultimate, concluding, hindmost
N. of the mountain Kailāsa in the Himālaya, the paradise of Śiva and residence of Kubera

dependent on another, subject to another's will, subservient, not one's own master; n. another's system or authoritative text; (in Buddhist philosophy) the dependent nature, that which arises from causes and conditions
free from the notion of 'mine', without self-interest or worldly attachment, unselfish, indifferent to possessions

the four-monthly Vedic sacrifices (the three seasonal sacrifices offered at the beginning of the three seasons of four months each); the four months of the rainy season and the observances kept during them
an exclamation of grief, alarm or lamentation (hāhā-bhūta, filled with cries of woe; hāhā-rava, a cry of distress); N. of a Gandharva (paired with Hūhū)
the breaker or render (of foes and mountains): an epithet of Indra
N. of Brahmā, the creator (also Viriñca; occasionally applied to Viṣṇu or Śiva)
serving to allay, pacify or alleviate (the humours), sedative, palliative (opposed to chedanīya, excisive remedies)
complete accomplishment, fulfilment, success, perfection; the highest perfection, final beatitude; complete preparation (of food, medicines, metals)
the closing of the eyes, shutting the eyelids; the closing of a flower; death (in lexicons)
silver, made of silver, silvery
a very fine cloth or garment woven from the inner bark of the dukūla plant, fine silk or muslin raiment
the cry, note or sound (of birds and animals), singing, warbling, howling (e.g. of jackals)
a eunuch, an impotent man, hermaphrodite (ṣaṇḍhatā, impotence; also of quicksilver whose potency is undeveloped, in alchemy)
anointing, smearing with oil or unguents, inunction, therapeutic oil-massage of the body
freedom from disease, health, healthiness, soundness of body; conferring health
the clearing-nut tree, Strychnos potatorum (whose nut is rubbed on vessels to clear and purify muddy water)
a washerman, cleaner or whitener of clothes (reckoned among the low castes); a dyer
leading or taking away, removal, dispelling (of fatigue, grief, sweat etc.); carrying off, abduction; (in arithmetic) subtraction
filling, filling up, completion, fulfilment, satisfaction, satiety; (in the Veda) a rewarding gift, bestowal
obstacle, hindrance, impediment, intervening obstruction (especially in Yoga, the nine distractions of the mind that obstruct concentration)
a heap, pile, mass, accumulation, store, stock, provisions (of grain, gold etc.); (in medicine) the simultaneous derangement or accumulation of all the humours
to arise, come into being, expand, develop; to pervade, fill, spread through; to suffice for, be adequate to, be a match for; vibhavataḥ, according to one's means or power
ritual impurity, defilement (especially the temporary impurity caused by a birth or a death among relatives, lasting a prescribed number of days)
N. of the celebrated princess of Avanti, daughter of king Pradyota and beloved wife of Udayana, king of Vatsa; also the heroine and title of Subandhu's romance
excepting, with the exception of, save, excluding, leaving out (at the end of compounds)
an ass, donkey
N. of a Vedic solar deity, the nourisher and protector of flocks, herds and roads (whose teeth were knocked out at Dakṣa's sacrifice); the sun
a she-camel; a large earthen jar or pot
suretyship, bail, security, the liability or obligation undertaken by a surety (for appearance, for confidence or for payment)
compassion, pity, mercy, tenderness (one of the four sublime states; in poetics the source of the pathetic sentiment)
quick, swift, prompt, willing; strong, powerful, mighty, victorious; rich, abundant (Vedic)
a contemptible man, coward, wretch, mean-spirited or low fellow
going away, departure, passing away, disappearance, cessation, removal (of a disease, a fault, a quality etc.)
the goddess dwelling in the Vindhya mountains, N. of Durgā
the tongue, the organ of taste
hanging down, hanging from, suspended, clinging to, resting or leaning upon (at the end of compounds); depending upon
property, wealth, possessions left at death, inheritance, heritage (riktha-bhāj, an heir, sharer of the estate)
wished, desired, longed for; n. wish, desire, the thing desired
unfortunate, unlucky, wretched, ill-fated; (of a woman) not loved by her husband, disliked (durbhagā)
a bed, couch, sofa, litter; a particular posture of meditation or sitting (squatting with the legs crossed and a band round knees and back)
faultless, blameless, irreproachable, unobjectionable
asleep, sleeping, slumbering; benumbed, insensible (of limbs); n. sleep, sleepiness, drowsiness
containing or bearing the Veda in his womb, the source of the Veda: an epithet of Brahmā (also applied to Viṣṇu and Śiva)
N. of a people and country in the south of India, the Dravidians (the Tamil land); (plural) its inhabitants
worldly, mundane, belonging to the world or to common life, ordinary, customary, popular, secular (as opposed to Vedic, ritual or spiritual); (m. plural) ordinary men, the common world
having deformed or uneven eyes: an epithet of Śiva (three-eyed); N. of one of the elephants that support the earth; N. of various Rākṣasas and Asuras
complete purification, purity, cleansing (of the body, the womb, metals etc.); clearing away, removal (of guilt or doubt); (in yoga) N. of the cakra of the throat
the hair of the head, a lock of hair, tress, curl; N. of a people and country in the south of India
a prefix to verbs and nouns meaning away, off, aside, back, forth (implying removal, reversal, aversion or deterioration, e.g. drive away, cast off)
the lord of beasts, a lion
white, yellowish-white, pale, shining, brilliant, fair-complexioned; m. a kind of wild ox or buffalo (Bos gaurus); white mustard (in lexicons)
setting out, departure, marching forth (of an army, a traveller, a procession); a course, method, system, school of doctrine or received line of teaching
a small village, hamlet, settlement (especially of herdsmen or wild tribes, as an Ābhīra or Śabara hamlet); a hut; a house-lizard (in lexicons)
dark-blue, blue-black, dark-coloured; m. the eye in a peacock's tail; darkness; a dark girdle-string
dust, a grain of dust, powder; pollen; sand
sowing, scattering (of seed); (in alchemy) the casting or projection of a powdered substance into or upon molten metal, cementation; shaving (in other contexts)
a cage (for birds or beasts), aviary; the skeleton, the ribs or rib-cage; a protective enclosure or armour-formula

the parting of the hair (especially of a woman; sīmantonnayana, the ceremonial parting of a pregnant wife's hair); a boundary, limit, landmark; (in anatomy) the sutures or junction-lines of the skull and skeleton
N. of one of the eight treasures of Kubera; an enormously high number; N. of a Nāga (serpent-demon); N. of one of the elephants of the quarters; a great (thousand-petalled) lotus
agreement, concurrence of opinion, assent, approval, sanction (of sages or authorities); esteem, respect, homage; N. of a sacred river (in Purāṇic lists)
breath, life, vital breath, spirit (gatāsu, vigatāsu, lifeless, whose breath is gone); (plural) the vital airs, the life-forces; (in astronomy) a unit of time equal to one respiration
a crab; the zodiacal sign Cancer; a pair of tongs or compasses, a crab-like grappling instrument; a kind of bamboo or sugar-cane (in lexicons)
to break or burst through, break out, sprout, shoot forth, spring up; udbhinna, burst forth, sprouted, broken out, appeared (as blood, horripilation, gems from a mine)
fire, the god of fire, Agni; (in the Veda) N. of the divine archer who guards the Soma
N. of the tyrannical king of Mathurā, uncle and enemy of Kṛṣṇa, slain by him; a drinking-vessel, cup or measure of bell-metal; bell-metal, white copper
thus, so, in this manner; truly, really, indeed (a Vedic particle, e.g. itthā mahān, truly great)
a wave, billow; a current, flow, flood; (figuratively) a wave or surge of grief, passion, existence (the six waves); a fold, undulation
loss, disappearance, deprivation; violation, neglect, transgression, interruption (of a vow, duty or observance); (in grammar) elision, the dropping of a sound, syllable or affix
a camp, military encampment, royal camp in the field; a tent
a dwelling, house, mansion, palace, abode; a temple; any chamber or apartment
a great lord, venerable or worshipful personage (a respectful title applied to gods, kings, sages and teachers; deva-bhaṭṭāraka, his majesty the king)
night; eating only at night (the nakta observance, a religious vow); naktāndha, night-blind
stupid, foolish, dull, silly; m. a fool, blockhead, dunce
the sacred fig-tree or peepal, Ficus religiosa (the aśvattha); n. its fruit or berry
holding, bearing, supporting, containing, sustaining, preserving (mostly at the end of compounds, e.g. bearing a name, sustaining virility); m. a receptacle, vessel

manifesting, indicating, making apparent, revealing, expressive; (in dramaturgy) expressive gesture, dramatic indication; (in rhetoric and philosophy) that which suggests or manifests (a latent meaning or entity)
a soldier, warrior, combatant, hired fighting-man; a servant, attendant, guard (of a king or of Yama)
turned towards the east, eastern, easterly (prācīna-vaṃśa, having the main beam pointing eastward); former, previous, prior; ancient, old
lotus-naveled: an epithet of Viṣṇu, from whose navel sprang the lotus bearing Brahmā
bright, brilliant, splendid, radiant, shining (Vedic); full of vigour, glorious
the coral tree, Erythrina indica, one of the five trees of paradise (its celestial flowers used in worship and adornment); also the arka or swallow-wort plant (in lexicons)
a dog; N. of a people and dynasty (the Kukkuras)
dwelling, abode, residence, habitation, house, nest; staying (especially for the night), a night's lodging (vasatiṃ gam, to take up one's abode)
a cloud, rain-cloud, thunder-cloud; N. of various men (a prince, a wrestler etc.)
a horn (of any animal); the tusk (of an elephant or boar); a claw (of a crab); śaśa-viṣāṇa, a hare's horn, the standard example of an impossibility
modified, altered, derived, secondary, produced by development or change (opposed to prākṛta, natural or original); n. modification, morbid change or derangement; a portent, prodigy
sandal, sandal-wood (literally born on the Malaya mountain); the sandal tree; sandal-paste
a creditor, money-lender (the party to whom a debt is owed, in law); a rich or wealthy man
family, descent, lineage, race, noble birth, high extraction; ancestors, forefathers; hereditary reputation or dignity
across, athwart, obliquely, sideways, beyond, over, through; apart, aside, out of sight, secretly (tiro-bhū, to disappear, vanish)
fearful, timid, cowardly, shy, afraid of (in compounds, e.g. fearing unrighteousness); (feminine vocative bhīru) O timid one (a tender address to women)
a plough (an eight-ox or six-ox plough reckoned as a measure of husbandry; hala-dhṛt, the plough-bearer, N. of Balarāma); (in grammar) a technical name comprising all the consonants
a barren or salty waste, desert, bare saline ground; a hollow, dell, water-worn channel
a cow-pen, cattle-shed, cow-station, fold, byre, enclosure or meeting-place of cattle and herdsmen

self-ruling, self-resplendent; an independent ruler, autonomous sovereign (a Vedic epithet of Indra and other gods); N. of one of the seven rays of the sun; a form of the supreme spirit (alongside virāj and samrāj)
a jackal
the hair on the body (of men and animals), body-hair, down, bristles (= roman); roma-lavaṇa, the salt of the Rumā salt-lake, Sāmbhar salt
